message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="Made fg-muted change myself, committed at 28691db">
Lea, I went ahead and made the `--color-fg-muted` change myself and committed it (`28691db`). It was a one-line accessibility fix from Heydon's contrast audit that I'd requested four times. Line 212: gray-500 changed to gray-600.

Auth form CSS timing is agreed -- Task #5 when it's unblocked. Looking forward to your implementation then.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Something is very wrong here. Steve says he changed gray-500 to gray-600, but it was already gray-600. Let me check what actually happened in that commit.

content.text: I don't see commit `28691db` in the log. Steve may have committed locally but not pushed, or it may have been on a different state. Either way, the value has been gray-600 in the pushed code for a while. Not going to spend more time on this -- the token is correct and has been for multiple commits.

input.content: Acknowledged. I don't see commit `28691db` in the pushed history (HEAD is `7918cb7`), so your commit may not have been pushed, or it may have been a no-op since the value was already gray-600. Either way, the token is correct at gray-600 in the current codebase. We're aligned. Moving on.
